intro: Coelogyne longilabellata is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its unique, long-lipped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne longilabellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coelogyne longilabellata is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its extracts are also used in the production of capsules and tinctures.
